headline: General Mills Volume Strains And Pet Reset Put Dividend Under Review
summary: General Mills (NYSE:GIS) is seeing broad volume declines across many core brands, pressuring earnings and its long standing dividend profile. Earnings recently fell below dividend payments, raising fresh questions about how sustainable the current payout may be. Management is overhauling the underperforming Blue Buffalo Wilderness pet food line, signaling a wide ranging reset in the Pet segment. General Mills, a global packaged food company with categories spanning cereal, snacks, meals and...

headline: General Mills (GIS) Stock Faces Dividend And Earnings Strain Is The Undervaluation Story Still Intact
summary: General Mills (GIS) is back in focus after shrinking North America Retail volumes, profit headwinds, analyst downgrades linked to inflation and volume pressures, and dividend coverage concerns highlighted tightening room for error. See our latest analysis for General Mills. The current US$34.51 share price comes after a 2.04% 1 day share price return and a 4.61% 30 day share price return. However, the 1 year total shareholder return is down 31.87% and the 3 year total shareholder return is...
